title: 5 stocks with consistent score improvement and upside potential of up to 26% in 1 year, according to analysts
summary: Here are three questions. First: When will the war end? Second: How long will it take to rebuild destroyed infrastructure? Third: How will the geopolitical situation pan out after? The answers to all three are up in the air. So, there is really no point in trying to figure out what will happen to the global equity markets. There is, however, one fact to consider: The market is in the habit of sharply pricing in events. And the short covering it witnessed on Thursday was perhaps the first indication of what is in the store. Our selected stocks for today depict a strong upward trajectory in their overall average score which is based on five key pillars: Earnings, fundamentals, relative valuation, risk, and price momentum. This implies that there has been a significant improvement in their market outlook in the given time frame.

title: Rethinking the role of AI in investing: What retail investors need in volatile markets
summary: AI in investing is often built for stable markets, limiting its usefulness during volatility. This piece argues that AI should enhance decision-making rather than replace human judgment. With rising retail participation in India, adaptive, context-driven AI tools focused on risk awareness and scenario analysis can significantly improve investment outcomes in uncertain market conditions.

title: AI evolution decoded: Ace investor Vijay Kedia explains it with a simple house-building analogy
summary: Veteran investor Vijay Kedia explains AI's evolution using a house-building analogy, from expert advice (LLMs) to autonomous action (agents), collaborative teams (multi-agent), human-level mastery (AGI), and beyond (superintelligence). This framework mirrors his investment philosophy: learn, earn, evolve. However, Kedia's portfolio faced a challenging FY26, with most holdings declining amid market pressures and geopolitical concerns.

title: HDFC Bank Q4 business update: Lender reports 15% YoY growth in deposits, advances jump 12%
